title: Global's Top Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Action In January 2026
content:
As we enter January 2026, global markets are experiencing mixed performances with small-cap and value stocks gaining momentum over large-cap counterparts, as evidenced by the S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 Indexes reaching all-time highs. Against this backdrop of economic indicators such as cooling core consumer prices and robust retail sales growth, identifying promising small-cap stocks becomes crucial for investors seeking potential opportunities in a dynamic market environment.

Top 10 Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Buying Globally

BARR 14.6x 1.7x 47.57% ★★★★☆☆ M&C Saatchi 22.6x 0.4x 47.51% ★★★★☆☆ Eastnine 12.2x 7.7x 48.08% ★★★★☆☆ Hung Hing Printing Group NA 0.4x 44.21% ★★★★☆☆ Ever Sunshine Services Group 6.8x 0.4x -453.79% ★★★☆☆☆ PSC 9.8x 0.4x 19.24% ★★★☆☆☆ Linc NA NA 9.35% ★★★☆☆☆

Click here to see the full list of 134 stocks from our Undervalued Global Small Caps With Insider Buying screener.

Let's review some notable picks from our screened stocks.

Virgin Australia Holdings

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★☆☆☆

Overview: Virgin Australia Holdings operates as a major airline company in Australia, providing domestic and international air travel services, with a market cap of A$3.5 billion.

Operations: The primary revenue stream is from its Airlines segment, contributing A$5.58 billion, with additional income from the Velocity segment at A$450 million. The gross profit margin has shown variability, reaching 30.33% recently. Operating expenses have been significant, with sales and marketing costs consistently present across periods.

PE: 5.1x

Virgin Australia Holdings, recently added to the S&P Global BMI Index, presents a compelling case in the small-cap category. Despite having no negative equity, VGN relies entirely on external borrowing for funding, which introduces higher risk compared to customer deposits. Insider confidence is evident with recent share purchases by insiders throughout 2025. While its financial position shows high debt levels, its inclusion in a global index could signal potential growth opportunities ahead.

Dive into the specifics of Virgin Australia Holdings here with our thorough valuation report. Gain insights into Virgin Australia Holdings' historical performance by reviewing our past performance report.ASX:VGN Ownership Breakdown as at Jan 2026

Neo Performance Materials

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Neo Performance Materials is a company engaged in producing and supplying advanced industrial materials such as magnetic powders, rare metals, and specialty chemicals, with a market capitalization of CAD $0.55 billion.

Story Continues

Operations: Magnequench and Chemicals & Oxides are significant revenue streams, contributing $193.10 million and $149.38 million respectively, with Rare Metals adding $156.42 million to the total revenue. The company's gross profit margin has shown fluctuations, recently recorded at 29.29% as of September 2025 after a period of variability from previous quarters.

PE: -92.4x

Neo Performance Materials has shown promising financial improvements, with Q3 2025 sales rising to US$122.21 million from US$111.28 million the previous year, and net income reaching US$1.36 million from a prior loss of US$2.63 million. Insider confidence is evident as Paul Mascarenas purchased 15,000 shares for approximately $185K in recent months. The company repurchased 386,068 shares for $3.9 million by November 2025 under its buyback program, signaling potential value recognition within the industry despite relying on external borrowing for funding needs and having no customer deposits as liabilities.

Get an in-depth perspective on Neo Performance Materials' performance by reading our valuation report here. Gain insights into Neo Performance Materials' past trends and performance with our Past report.TSX:NEO Share price vs Value as at Jan 2026

Pro Real Estate Investment Trust

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★☆☆☆

Overview: Pro Real Estate Investment Trust operates in the real estate sector focusing on office and retail properties, with a market cap of CA$1.23 billion.

Operations: The company generates revenue primarily from retail and office segments, with a significant portion attributed to segment adjustments. Over recent periods, the net profit margin has shown variability, reaching as high as 1.50% in September 2022 before declining to 0.27% by September 2025. Operating expenses include costs such as general and administrative expenses which have fluctuated but remained a notable component of total expenses.

PE: 11.9x

Pro Real Estate Investment Trust, a smaller company in the real estate sector, recently expanded its portfolio with a $5.4 million acquisition of an industrial property in Winnipeg, enhancing its asset base to 23 properties. Insider confidence is evident as Co-Founder Gordon Lawlor purchased 20,000 shares for approximately C$108,241. Despite challenges such as earnings decline and reliance on external borrowing for funding, recent dividend affirmations and strategic acquisitions suggest potential growth avenues in the future.

title: Global's Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Action In December 2025
content:
In December 2025, global markets are navigating a complex landscape marked by mixed economic signals and varied performances across major indices. While the Russell 2000 Index saw a decline of 0.86%, reflecting challenges in the small-cap sector, broader market sentiment was buoyed by positive inflation data and strong earnings reports from key players like Micron Technology. In this environment, identifying promising small-cap stocks involves looking for companies that demonstrate resilience amid economic fluctuations and have strategic insider actions that may signal confidence in their future prospects.

Top 10 Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Buying Globally

Click here to see the full list of 148 stocks from our Undervalued Global Small Caps With Insider Buying screener.

Below we spotlight a couple of our favorites from our exclusive screener.

Virgin Australia Holdings

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★☆☆☆

Overview: Virgin Australia Holdings operates as a major airline company in Australia, offering domestic and international air travel services, with a market cap of A$2.5 billion.

Operations: The company's primary revenue streams are from its Airlines and Velocity segments, with a significant contribution of A$5.58 billion and A$450 million, respectively. The gross profit margin has shown an upward trend in recent periods, reaching 30.33% as of June 2024. Operating expenses include notable allocations towards sales and marketing, which were A$421.4 million in the latest quarter ending December 2024.

PE: 5.4x

Virgin Australia Holdings, a notable player among smaller companies, recently joined the S&P Global BMI Index on December 20, 2025. This inclusion could enhance visibility and attract new investors. Despite carrying significant debt due to reliance on external borrowing, insider confidence is evident with purchase activities over the past year. Such moves suggest belief in potential growth or value realization. The company's financial position reflects no negative equity but highlights higher risk funding sources without customer deposits.

Take a closer look at Virgin Australia Holdings' potential here in our valuation report. Review our historical performance report to gain insights into Virgin Australia Holdings''s past performance.

Story Continues

ASX:VGN Ownership Breakdown as at Dec 2025

Killam Apartment REIT

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★☆

Overview: Killam Apartment REIT is a Canadian real estate investment trust primarily engaged in owning, operating, and developing apartment buildings, commercial properties, and manufactured home communities with a market capitalization of approximately CA$2.16 billion.

Operations: Killam Apartment REIT generates revenue primarily from its apartment segment, with additional contributions from commercial properties and manufactured home communities. The company has shown a gross profit margin reaching 66.57% as of the latest period. Operating expenses have fluctuated, but recent data indicates an increase to CA$22.75 million for the quarter ending June 2025.

PE: 3.7x

Killam Apartment REIT, a smaller player in the real estate sector, has shown resilience despite financial challenges. Their third-quarter 2025 sales reached C$98.47 million, up from C$93.79 million the previous year, though net income decreased to C$41.86 million from C$62.73 million. Despite relying solely on external borrowing for funding and having interest payments not fully covered by earnings, insider confidence remains high with recent purchases indicating potential long-term faith in its prospects.

Dive into the specifics of Killam Apartment REIT here with our thorough valuation report. Understand Killam Apartment REIT's track record by examining our Past report.TSX:KMP.UN Share price vs Value as at Dec 2025

Valeura Energy

Simply Wall St Value Rating: ★★★★★★

Overview: Valeura Energy is an oil and gas exploration and production company with a focus on developing assets, primarily generating revenue from its operations in this sector, and it has a market capitalization of approximately $2.4 billion.

Operations: The primary revenue stream is from oil and gas exploration and production, with a reported revenue of $667.89 million. The cost of goods sold (COGS) for the same period was $187.35 million, resulting in a gross profit margin of 71.95%. Operating expenses amounted to $262.02 million, which includes significant depreciation and amortization costs at $185.54 million, impacting overall profitability despite positive net income figures in recent periods.

PE: 2.6x

Valeura Energy, a smaller company in the energy sector, recently announced a share repurchase program to buy back up to 6.3 million shares, reflecting insider confidence in its potential. For Q3 2025, they reported US$159 million in revenue and US$15.81 million net income, reversing last year's loss. Production guidance for 2025 remains strong with daily oil output expected between 23 and 25.5 mbbls/d. Despite high-risk funding from external borrowing and projected earnings decline over three years, their recent performance suggests resilience amidst industry challenges.
